intro: Muraltia polyphylla, commonly known as the five-leafed Muraltia, is a succulent shrub native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and wounds, due to its potential health benefits, such as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Muraltia polyphylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Muraltia polyphylla is often used in traditional medicine and is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in topical applications, such as ointments and creams, to harness its health benefits.
